properties.name: Welsh
properties.fact: Welsh is the strongest surviving Celtic language, with a continuous literary tradition dating back to the 6th century.

properties.name: Scottish Gaelic
properties.fact: Scottish Gaelic is concentrated in the Hebrides and has declined sharply since the 19th century clearances.

properties.name: Irish
properties.fact: Irish is a compulsory subject in Irish schools and holds official status in the Republic of Ireland and the EU.
